user.title: Welcome
user.description:
EyeBond Local will help you add an inverter with as little manual setup as possible.

{welcome_hint}

data_description.connection_type: Choose how Home Assistant should reach the inverter.

collector_network.title: Collector Network Setup
collector_network.description:
Is the collector already on the same network as Home Assistant? If yes, choose the first option.

If not, two ways to get it online:
- **Bluetooth** (second option) — the easiest path on newer collectors. Needs Bluetooth on the Home Assistant side: a local adapter or an ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy near the collector.
- **The collector's own Wi-Fi** — connect the Home Assistant host to the access point the collector broadcasts (the SSID contains the collector PN, the password is usually `12345678`), then choose the first option.

After the device is added, you can move the collector onto your normal Wi-Fi from the integration settings.

menu_options.auto: Yes, the collector is already on my network
menu_options.bluetooth_setup: No, connect the collector to Wi-Fi using Bluetooth first

auto.title: Choose Scan Interface
auto.description: Choose the Home Assistant network interface (the one on the same subnet as your inverter), then start the scan.

data_description.server_ip: The network interface that the collector will connect back to. Pick the one on the same subnet as your inverter.

bluetooth_setup.title: Bluetooth Wi-Fi Setup
bluetooth_setup.description: Use Bluetooth only to put the collector on your Wi-Fi network. After provisioning finishes, EyeBond Local will return to the normal network scan.

data_description.ble_address: Pick a discovered collector. If discovery does not find one, enter the collector Bluetooth address manually. Home Assistant Bluetooth proxies also participate here.
data_description.wifi_ssid: Choose one of the Wi-Fi networks that the collector can currently see over Bluetooth, or enter a hidden/custom SSID manually.
data_description.wifi_password: Password for the Wi-Fi SSID that will be written to the collector.
data_description.ble_action: Choose whether this submit should refresh the collector list, refresh the Wi-Fi list for the selected collector, or apply the entered Wi-Fi settings to that collector.

deep_scan.title: Deep Scan

scanning.title: Scanning Network

data_description.result_key: Pick a found device to add it, or pick a follow-up action.

change_scan_interface.title: Change Scan Interface
change_scan_interface.description: Choose the Home Assistant interface below, then run the scan again.

data_description.server_ip: Pick a different network interface to scan from.

data_description.result_key: Choose the inverter you want to add to Home Assistant.

data_description.driver_match_key: If you are not sure, keep the recommended option.

data_description.collector_operation_mode: SmartESS cloud + Home Assistant keeps the existing collector server setting. Home Assistant only changes the collector callback endpoint to this Home Assistant listener.
data_description.poll_mode: Automatic lets EyeBond Local choose a safe refresh interval from device response time. Manual uses the interval below.
data_description.poll_interval: How often to read sensor values from the inverter after the device is added.

data_description.collector_operation_mode: SmartESS cloud + Home Assistant keeps the existing collector server setting. Home Assistant only changes the collector callback endpoint to this Home Assistant listener.
data_description.poll_mode: Automatic lets EyeBond Local choose a safe refresh interval from device response time. Manual uses the interval below.
data_description.poll_interval: How often to read sensor values from the inverter after the device is added.

confirm_poll_interval.title: Manual refresh interval
confirm_poll_interval.description: Manual refresh mode uses the exact interval below.

data_description.poll_interval: Manual mode only. This is the target start-to-start refresh interval. If the device needs longer to answer, EyeBond Local will warn about high poll utilization; use Automatic mode to let the integration choose a safe interval.

smartess_cloud_assist.title: SmartESS Cloud Assist

data_description.username: The SmartESS account name used by the mobile app.
data_description.password: The SmartESS account password. It is used only for this live onboarding assist step.

manual.title: Manual Setup
manual.description: For setups the auto-scan cannot reach: a remote collector, VPN/NAT, or a known fixed address. The settings are probed first — if the device is not confirmed, you can adjust them and retry on the next screen.

data_description.server_ip: Local Home Assistant IPv4 address used to bind the EyeBond TCP listener. For normal LAN setups, choose the interface on the same subnet as the collector.
data_description.collector_ip: Collector IPv4 address to probe directly. Leave empty for local broadcast discovery. For remote or NAT setups, set the public or forwarded IPv4 that reaches the collector.
data_description.driver_hint: Auto-detect works for most inverters. Override only if instructed.

advanced_connection.name: Advanced connection settings
advanced_connection.description: Only needed for remote, VPN, or NAT setups. For a normal home network the defaults are correct — you can leave everything here untouched.

data_description.tcp_port: Local TCP listener port on Home Assistant. Default: 8899.
data_description.advertised_server_ip: Public or forwarded IP the collector should call back to. Leave empty for normal home networks (the local address above is used).
data_description.advertised_tcp_port: Forwarded external port for the callback. Leave empty unless your router forwards a different external port.
data_description.udp_port: Port used to discover the collector on the network. Default: 58899 — change only if instructed.
data_description.discovery_target: IPv4 address that receives discovery packets. Use subnet broadcast for local LAN, or a specific remote/NAT IPv4 for unicast discovery.
data_description.discovery_interval: How often to send discovery redirects while waiting for the collector.
data_description.heartbeat_interval: How often Home Assistant pings the collector to keep the connection alive.

advanced_setup.title: Advanced setup
advanced_setup.description: Use these only if the automatic scan did not find your inverter.

progress.scanning_network: Scanning the local network for EyeBond collectors…

error.cannot_autodetect: Auto-detection did not find any devices. Check the network interface and try again, or use manual setup.
error.ble_unavailable: Bluetooth provisioning is not available on this Home Assistant host. If the host has no local BLE adapter, add an ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy near the collector and try again.
error.ble_scan_failed: Bluetooth scan failed on this Home Assistant host. If the collector is only reachable from nearby, try again with an ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy close to it.
error.ble_wifi_ssid_invalid: Enter the Wi-Fi network name to send over Bluetooth.
error.ble_wifi_password_invalid: Enter the Wi-Fi password to send over Bluetooth.
error.ble_wifi_scan_failed: Found the collector over Bluetooth, but could not read its Wi-Fi list — the Bluetooth link is probably weak. Move the collector or the adapter closer (or place an ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy nearby) and try again.
error.ble_provision_failed: Bluetooth provisioning did not complete successfully. Check the collector, adapter permissions, and Wi-Fi credentials, then try again.
error.collector_endpoint_write_failed: Could not write the Home Assistant callback endpoint to the collector.
error.invalid_selection: The selected detection result is no longer available.
error.already_added_candidate: This detected device is already configured and cannot be added again.
error.smartess_cloud_assist_failed: SmartESS cloud assist did not complete. See the explanation above and try again.
error.inverter_link_down: The collector responded, but the inverter link is down (identity registers read as zeros). Check the inverter power and the collector cable, then rescan.

abort.already_configured: This collector or inverter is already configured.

error.collector_wifi_read_failed: Could not read Wi-Fi status from the collector.
error.collector_wifi_write_failed: Could not write Wi-Fi settings to the collector.
error.collector_wifi_ssid_not_ascii: This collector accepts only ASCII Wi-Fi network names on the local management path.
error.collector_wifi_password_not_ascii: This collector accepts only ASCII Wi-Fi passwords on the local management path.
error.collector_wifi_apply_not_confirmed: Confirm that applying Wi-Fi settings may temporarily disconnect the collector.
error.collector_uart_read_failed: Could not read UART settings from the collector.
error.collector_uart_write_failed: Could not write UART settings to the collector.
error.collector_uart_apply_not_confirmed: Confirm that changing the UART speed may temporarily stop inverter communication.
error.collector_uart_runtime_unavailable: This collector hardware cannot change UART speed at runtime.
error.proxy_capture_action_failed: The capture action failed. Check the Home Assistant log for details.
error.diagnostic_commands_required: Paste at least one diagnostic command.
error.diagnostic_commands_unavailable: The integration runtime is not loaded, so diagnostic commands cannot run.
error.diagnostic_commands_failed: The diagnostic scenario failed unexpectedly. Check the Home Assistant log.
error.shadow_learning_failed: Control discovery action failed. Check details and confirmations, then try again.
error.invalid_selection: The selected option is no longer available.

init.title: EyeBond Local Options
init.description: Choose what you want to manage for this entry.{bridge_note}

collector_wifi.title: Change Collector Wi-Fi

data.confirm_collector_wifi_apply: I understand that the collector may temporarily disconnect while applying the new Wi-Fi settings

data_description.wifi_password: The current password is never read back from the collector. Enter it only when applying new settings.
data_description.confirm_collector_wifi_apply: After changing the network settings, it is recommended to remove this device from Home Assistant and add it again so the collector reconnects using its new IP address.

collector_uart.title: Change inverter UART speed

data.confirm_collector_uart_apply: I understand that changing the UART speed may temporarily stop inverter communication

data_description.collector_uart_baudrate: The bridge keeps the current framing and changes only the baud rate. PI30/Voltronic devices commonly use 2400; SMG/Modbus devices commonly use 9600.
data_description.confirm_collector_uart_apply: If the speed is wrong, Home Assistant may keep seeing the collector but stop receiving inverter data until the speed is corrected.

data_description.poll_mode: Automatic lets EyeBond Local choose a safe refresh interval from device response time. Manual uses the interval below.
data_description.poll_interval: Manual mode only. This is the target start-to-start refresh interval. If the device needs longer to answer, EyeBond Local will warn about high poll utilization; use Automatic mode to let the integration choose a safe interval.
data_description.collector_operation_mode: SmartESS + HA keeps the collector visible in the SmartESS app while Home Assistant also talks to it locally. Home Assistant only makes that collector reconnect to Home Assistant.
data_description.control_mode: Auto enables tested controls when detection confidence is high. Read-only disables all controls. Full enables everything.

connection.name: Connection settings
connection.description: Only needed for remote, VPN, or NAT setups. For a normal home network the defaults are correct — you can leave everything here untouched.

data_description.server_ip: Local Home Assistant IPv4 address used to bind the EyeBond TCP listener.
data_description.collector_ip: Leave empty for local broadcast discovery. For remote or NAT setups, set the public or forwarded IPv4 that reaches the collector.
data_description.tcp_port: Local TCP listener port on Home Assistant. Default: 8899.
data_description.advertised_server_ip: Public or forwarded IP the collector should call back to. Leave empty for normal home networks (the local address above is used).
data_description.advertised_tcp_port: Forwarded external port for the callback. Leave empty unless your router forwards a different external port.
data_description.udp_port: Port used to discover the collector on the network. Default: 58899 — change only if instructed.
data_description.discovery_target: IPv4 address that receives discovery packets. Use subnet broadcast for local LAN, or a specific remote/NAT IPv4 for unicast discovery.
data_description.discovery_interval: How often to send discovery redirects while waiting for the collector.
data_description.heartbeat_interval: How often Home Assistant pings the collector to keep the connection alive.

runtime_poll_interval.title: Manual refresh interval
runtime_poll_interval.description: Manual refresh mode uses the exact interval below.

data_description.poll_interval: Manual mode only. This is the target start-to-start refresh interval. If the device needs longer to answer, EyeBond Local will warn about high poll utilization; use Automatic mode to let the integration choose a safe interval.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics And Experimental Metadata

diagnostic_commands.title: Diagnostic Commands

data_description.diagnostic_commands: Paste the complete multiline scenario, including driver or address overrides when provided.
data_description.diagnostic_stop_on_error: Disable this only when later commands should still run after a failed command.
data_description.diagnostic_publish_download_copy: Copies the redacted shareable result to Home Assistant /local. Leave disabled unless you intend to download or share it.
data_description.diagnostic_result: Read-only output from the latest run. Copy it or use the shareable download link above.

data.shadow_learning_confirm_cloud_write: Yes, briefly check which extra sensors and controls this device supports

data_description.shadow_learning_confirm_cloud_write: Home Assistant briefly checks this device through its cloud service to discover extra controls and sensor evidence. The device's settings are not changed.

data_description.username: Used only for the current device-support discovery action.
data_description.password: Used only for the current device-support discovery action.

data_description.enabled_controls: Tick the extra controls you want in Home Assistant. Risky ones are left off by default.

data.result_action: What would you like to do?

data_description.proxy_capture_duration_minutes: Minutes before Home Assistant automatically stops proxy capture and restores the collector connection.
data_description.proxy_capture_live_log_view: Scroll inside this read-only field to review the current session log. Changes here are ignored.

create_support_package.title: Create Support Archive
create_support_package.description:
Builds the support archive in one step. Saved cloud evidence is included automatically; choose **{refresh_mode_label}** to fetch a fresh copy with your cloud account login.

| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| Collector PN | {collector_pn} |
| Current cloud evidence | {cloud_evidence_path} |
| Archive plan | {smartess_archive_plan_summary} |

data_description.smartess_cloud_mode: Choose whether to use saved cloud evidence, refresh it now inside this step, or build the archive without it.
data_description.username: Only needed when fetching or refreshing cloud evidence in this step.
data_description.password: Only needed when fetching or refreshing cloud evidence in this step. It is used only for this live action.

rollback_local_metadata.title: Rollback Local Metadata
rollback_local_metadata.description:
Removes the active local override files below and reloads the device on built-in metadata. Inactive drafts and saved evidence are not touched.

| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| Active override files | {rollback_target_count} |
| Profile override | {rollback_profile_path} |
| Schema override | {rollback_schema_path} |
